questions.question:
Answer with the option letter only. Which column order do the visible portico columns most closely resemble?
A. Doric
B. Ionic
C. Corinthian
D. Tuscan
Answer with yes or no only. Is the triangular pediment visible in this image?

Answer with the option letter only. Approximately how many large portico columns are visible in the image?
A. 0–5
B. 6–10
C. 11–20
D. More than 20
What does the Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image mean?
questions.expected: It states that Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, built it when he was consul for the third time.
questions.rgb_answer: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark reads "MAGRIPPA L. F. COS. TRIVM. FECIT," which translates to "Magrippa, son of Lucius, made this third time." This inscription indicates that the structure was built or restored by a person named Magrippa, who was the son of Lucius and held the title of Consul Trivum, suggesting that he was a high-ranking official in Roman society at the time. The term "FECIT" is a common way to end inscriptions in Latin, meaning "he/she/it made."

gt_answers.Small: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image reads 'MAGISTERIUM COSECTATIS', which translates to 'The Master of the Scribe' or 'The Master of the Copyist'.
gt_answers.Medium: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image reads "MAGRIPPA L. F. COS TERTIVS IMP ECIT," which translates to "Magrippa, son of Lucius Furius, third consul, built this."
gt_answers.Large: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image reads "MAGRIPPAFVLIVSIVLIMESTERIETC". This translates to "Magrippa, son of Publius, built this temple for the gods." It indicates that the temple was constructed by a person named Magrippa, who was the son of Publius, and it was dedicated to the gods.

rendered_answers.Small: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image reads "
rendered_answers.Medium: The Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image reads "MAGISTERIUM TERTIUM," which translates to "Third Masterpiece."
rendered_answers.Large: The Latin inscription above the columns reads "MAGISTERIUM COS TERTIUM," which translates to "Third Master's House."
